6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.HigherDirectImage
8	
9	/-!
10	# Čech computation of the higher direct images `Rⁱ f_*` (unconditional)
11	
12	This file constructs the higher derived direct images `Rⁱ f_* F` for `i ≥ 1`
13	**without appealing to injective resolutions** in the category of sheaves of
14	modules. The companion `Cohomology/HigherDirectImage.lean` defines `Rⁱ f_*` as a
15	right derived functor, which requires the ambient category of `O_X`-modules to
16	have enough injectives — a property not currently available for sheaves of
17	modules over a sheaf of rings whose value category varies over the site. The
18	Čech approach developed here sidesteps the issue: it computes `Rⁱ f_* F` as the
19	cohomology of an explicit complex built from the pushforwards of `F` over the
20	finite intersections of an affine open cover, producing an **unconditional**
21	construction of `Rⁱ f_*` for quasi-coherent `F` and separated quasi-compact `f`.
22	
23	Throughout, `f : X ⟶ S` is a quasi-compact, separated morphism of schemes (so
24	all finite intersections of an affine open cover of `X` are again affine), and
25	`F : X.Modules` is a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module. A base change of `f` along
26	`g : S' ⟶ S` is recorded by a cartesian square
27	```
28	  X' --g'--> X
29	  |f'        |f
30	  v          v
31	  S' --g---> S
32	```
33	with `F' = (g')^* F` the pullback of `F` to `X'`.
34	
35	The six main declarations are:
36	
37	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve` — the (augmented) Čech nerve of an affine open
38	  cover, an augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules.
39	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex` — the relative Čech complex in `QCoh(S)`, a
40	  cochain complex of `O_S`-modules whose degree-`p` term is the product of the
41	  pushforwards of `F` over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections of the cover.
42	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechAcyclic.affine` — Čech acyclicity on affines: the Čech
43	  complex of a standard cover of an affine scheme has vanishing cohomology in
44	  all positive degrees (Serre vanishing for quasi-coherent sheaves on affines).
45	* `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` — the cohomology of the
46	  relative Čech complex is canonically isomorphic to `Rⁱ f_* F` wherever the
47	  derived functor is defined.
48	
49	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`.
50	
51	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech cohomology of quasi-coherent
52	sheaves and §Quasi-coherence of higher direct images; Tags 02KE
53	(`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent`), 02KG
54	(`lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero`).
55	-/
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	open Scheme.Modules
64	
65	variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
66	
67	/-- **Čech nerve of an affine open cover** (Stacks, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech
68	cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves).
69	
70	For a scheme `X`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰 : X = ⋃ Uᵢ` and a quasi-coherent
71	sheaf `F`, the *Čech nerve* is the augmented cosimplicial object of
72	`O_X`-modules whose object in simplicial degree `p` is the product, over the
73	`(p+1)`-tuples `(i₀,…,i_p)` of indices, of the direct images
74	`(j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})` of the restriction of `F` to the
75	`(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{i₀…i_p} = U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{i_p}` along the open
76	immersion `j_{i₀…i_p} : U_{i₀…i_p} ↪ X`. Faces are the restriction maps that
77	omit one index, degeneracies repeat one index, and the augmentation in degree
78	`-1` is `F` itself on all of `X`. When `X` is separated each intersection
79	`U_{i₀…i_p}` is affine.
80	
81	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
82	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
83	noncomputable def CechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
84	    CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules :=
85	  -- Construction (Stacks): the augmented cosimplicial object
86	  -- `[p] ↦ ∏_{(i₀,…,i_p)} (j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})` with faces the
87	  -- index-omitting restriction maps, degeneracies the index-repeating maps, and
88	  -- augmentation `F`. Requires the nerve of the cover (intersection products of
89	  -- pushforwards) as a functor out of `SimplexCategory`, currently absent from
90	  -- Mathlib for `Scheme.Modules`.
91	  sorry
92	
93	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scheme-level Čech nerve backbone
94	
95	The genuine construction of the {\v C}ech nerve `CechNerve` factors through two
96	ingredients that are independent of one another:
97	
98	* a *geometric* backbone — the augmented {\v C}ech nerve of the cover, an
99	  augmented simplicial **scheme** over `X` (the iterated fibre powers of
100	  `∐ᵢ Uᵢ` over `X`), which exists unconditionally because `Scheme` has all
101	  finite limits; and
102	* a *push-pull* functor `(Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`, that
103	  turns the simplicial scheme over `X` into the cosimplicial `O_X`-module
104	  `CechNerve`.
105	
106	The backbone (`coverArrow`, `coverCechNerve`) is built here axiom-clean. The
107	push-pull functor is the remaining gap: its `map_comp` requires the
108	`pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` coherence isomorphisms (the same coherence
109	quagmire active in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`), so `CechNerve` itself is
110	left as the single genuine hole.
111	
112	Independently of the nerve, the passage *from* an augmented cosimplicial
113	`O_X`-module *to* the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` is pure,
114	coherence-free plumbing (`relativeCechComplexOfNerve`): forget the augmentation,
115	push forward along `f` via `CosimplicialObject.whiskering`, and take the
116	alternating coface-map cochain complex. We record it here so that `CechComplex`
117	is *defined* in terms of `CechNerve` — closing `CechNerve` axiom-clean
118	immediately yields an axiom-clean `CechComplex`. -/
119	
120	/-- The arrow `∐ᵢ Uᵢ ⟶ X` (`Sigma.desc 𝒰.f`) attached to an open cover `𝒰` of a
121	scheme `X`. Its augmented {\v C}ech nerve is the geometric backbone of the
122	relative {\v C}ech complex. Project-local: packages the cover as a single arrow
123	so the existing `Arrow.augmentedCechNerve` machinery applies. -/
124	noncomputable def coverArrow (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) : Arrow Scheme.{u} :=
125	  Arrow.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)
126	
127	/-- The augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an open cover `𝒰`, as an augmented
128	simplicial scheme over `X`: in simplicial degree `p` it is the `(p+1)`-fold
129	fibre power of `∐ᵢ Uᵢ` over `X`, i.e. `∐_{(i₀,…,i_p)} U_{i₀} ×ₓ ⋯ ×ₓ U_{i_p}`,
130	with augmentation the cover map to `X`. Exists unconditionally because `Scheme`
131	has all finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks used by
132	`Arrow.augmentedCechNerve`). Project-local geometric backbone for `CechNerve`. -/
133	noncomputable def coverCechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
134	    SimplicialObject.Augmented Scheme.{u} :=
135	  (coverArrow 𝒰).augmentedCechNerve
136	
137	/-! ### Push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules` — object and morphism bricks
138	
139	The geometric backbone above is lifted to a cosimplicial `O_X`-module by the
140	*relative-direct-image functor on the over-category*
141	```
142	  G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules,   (Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F,
143	```
144	sending an `X`-scheme `p : Y ⟶ X` to the pushforward along `p` of the pullback
145	`p^* F`. We record here the two *pre-coherence* bricks of `G` — its action on
146	objects (`pushPullObj`, the planner's `Gobj`) and on morphisms (`pushPullMap`,
147	the planner's `Gmap`) — both axiom-clean and free of any functor law. The functor
148	laws `G(𝟙) = 𝟙` and `G(g ≫ h) = G(h) ≫ G(g)` are a *consumer* of the
149	pushforward/pullback composition coherence (`pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` and
150	their unitor/pentagon identities) and are deferred; see the note after
151	`pushPullMap`. -/
152	
153	/-- The object map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`,
154	`(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. Sends an `X`-scheme `Y` (with structure map `Y.hom : Y.left
155	⟶ X`) to the pushforward along `Y.hom` of the pullback of `F`. Project-local
156	object brick of the {\v C}ech push–pull functor (the planner's `Gobj`). -/
157	noncomputable def pushPullObj (F : X.Modules) (Y : Over X) : X.Modules :=
158	  (pushforward Y.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)
159	
160	/-- The morphism map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`. For a
161	morphism `g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁` of `X`-schemes (so `g.left ≫ Y₁.hom = Y₂.hom`, the
162	over-triangle `Over.w g`), the contravariant functor produces the restriction
163	comparison `pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂`. It is the three-step composite:
164	the unit `η` of the adjunction `pullback g.left ⊣ pushforward g.left`, the
165	pushforward comparison `(pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, and the pushforward
166	of the pullback comparison `(pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, glued by two
167	`eqToHom` transports along the over-triangle `Over.w g`. No functor law is used:
168	this is a reusable pre-coherence brick (the planner's `Gmap`). -/
169	noncomputable def pushPullMap (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) :
170	    pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂ :=
171	  (pushforward Y₁.hom).map
172	      ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g.left).unit.app
173	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
174	    (pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app
175	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
176	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward q).obj
177	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)))
178	      (Over.w g)) ≫
179	    (pushforward Y₂.hom).map ((pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app F) ≫
180	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward Y₂.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F))
181	      (Over.w g))
182	
183	/- **The functor laws `pushPullMap_id` / `pushPullMap_comp`.**
184	Assembling `pushPullObj` / `pushPullMap` into the functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤
185	X.Modules` requires
186	```
187	  pushPullMap_id   : pushPullMap F (𝟙 Y) = 𝟙 (pushPullObj F Y)          -- DONE (below)
188	  pushPullMap_comp : pushPullMap F (g ≫ h) = pushPullMap F h ≫ pushPullMap F g  -- remaining
189	```
190	Both are pure pushforward/pullback *coherence* statements with no sectionwise
191	content of their own. The route is the adjunction-mate calculus: the
192	`unit ≫ pushforwardComp.hom` head of `pushPullMap` is governed by `unit_conjugateEquiv`
193	and `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` / `conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom`, and the `eqToHom`
194	transports are discharged against the pseudofunctor unitor/pentagon identities
195	(`pseudofunctor_right_unitality` for `id`, `pseudofunctor_associativity` for `comp`, all in
196	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean`).
197	
198	`pushPullMap_id` is closed axiom-clean below. A plain `simp` (even with the coherence lemmas)
199	makes no progress on the head `unit ≫ pushforwardComp.hom`, and the sectionwise `ext` route
200	exposes the pullback adjunction unit (not sectionwise trivial); the working proof instead routes
201	through `unit_conjugateEquiv` of the identity adjunction (`star`), the right-unitality of the
202	pullback comparison (`hru`), and a `Scheme.Modules.hom_ext; intro U; rfl` collapse of the
203	sectionwise-trivial pushforward coercion (`hpf`). `pushPullMap_comp` follows the same template
204	with `pseudofunctor_associativity` (the pentagon) in place of right-unitality — see the comment
205	above its (currently unfilled) statement. -/
206	
207	/-! ### Functor laws of the push–pull functor `G` -/
208	
209	/-- Identity law of the push–pull functor `G`. -/
210	lemma pushPullMap_id (F : X.Modules) (Y : Over X) :
211	    pushPullMap F (𝟙 Y) = 𝟙 (pushPullObj F Y) := by
212	  -- `star`: the unit-triangle for the identity adjunction `pullback 𝟙 ⊣ pushforward 𝟙`.
213	  have star := unit_conjugateEquiv (Adjunction.id (C := Y.left.Modules))
214	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 Y.left))
215	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)
216	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom] at star
217	  simp only [Adjunction.id_unit, NatTrans.id_app, Functor.id_obj] at star
218	  -- `hru`: right-unitality of the pullback pseudofunctor, applied at `F`.
219	  have hru := Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_right_unitality (X := Y.left) (f := Y.hom)
220	  have hru2 := congrArg (fun t => NatTrans.app t F) hru
221	  simp only [NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.rightUnitor_hom_app] at hru2
222	  have hpf : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (𝟙 Y.left) Y.hom).hom.app
223	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)) ≫
224	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
225	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)))
226	        (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
227	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y.left).hom.app
228	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F))) := by
229	    apply Scheme.Modules.hom_ext
230	    intro U; rfl
231	  -- the unit zig-zag for the identity adjunction collapses on `M`
232	  have hzig : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 Y.left)).unit.app
233	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) ≫
234	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y.left).hom.app
235	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)) ≫
236	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) =
237	      𝟙 ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) := by
238	    have hnat := (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y.left).hom.naturality
239	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F))
240	    simp only [Functor.id_map] at hnat
241	    erw [← hnat, ← reassoc_of% star]
242	    exact Iso.inv_hom_id_app _ _
243	  -- the pullback comparison + the over-triangle transport collapse via right-unitality
244	  have hib_inner : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (𝟙 Y.left) Y.hom).hom.app F ≫
245	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
246	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) := by
247	    rw [eqToHom_app] at hru2
248	    rw [← hru2, ← Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_app]; simp
249	  have hib : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map
250	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (𝟙 Y.left) Y.hom).hom.app F) ≫
251	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).obj
252	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
253	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map
254	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)) := by
255	    have he : eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).obj
256	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
257	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map
258	          (eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)
259	            (Category.id_comp Y.hom))) := by
260	      rw [eqToHom_map]
261	    rw [he, ← Functor.map_comp]; exact congrArg _ hib_inner
262	  -- assemble
263	  simp only [pushPullMap, Over.id_left]
264	  erw [reassoc_of% hpf, hib, ← Functor.map_comp]
265	  erw [hzig, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]; rfl

267	/- **Composition law of the push–pull functor `G` (contravariant) — remaining.**
268	The identity law `pushPullMap_id` above is closed axiom-clean by the mate calculus
269	(`unit_conjugateEquiv` + `conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom` + `pseudofunctor_right_unitality`).
270	The composition law
271	```
272	  pushPullMap_comp : pushPullMap F (g ≫ h) = pushPullMap F h ≫ pushPullMap F g
273	```
274	follows the *same* template one step up — but with the pentagon
275	`Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := g.left) (g := h.left) (h := Y₁.hom)`
276	(verified to typecheck) in place of `pseudofunctor_right_unitality`, the composite-adjunction
277	unit expansion `Adjunction.comp_unit_app` for `η_{g.left ≫ h.left}`, and
278	`Adjunction.unit_naturality` to slide the inner unit past the outer pushforward. The pushforward
279	coercions (`pushforwardComp ≫ eqToHom`) collapse sectionwise by `Scheme.Modules.hom_ext; intro U; rfl`
280	exactly as the `hpf` helper of `pushPullMap_id`. It is a longer (~150-LOC) pentagon calculation and is
281	left for a focused follow-up pass; assembling `pushPullFunctor` needs it together with `pushPullMap_id`.
282	
283	The mate core that splits the composite unit is isolated as the reusable lemma
284	`pushPull_unit_mate` below (axiom-clean): it rewrites the head
285	`p_*(η^f) ≫ pushforwardComp` as `η^{f≫p} ≫ (f≫p)_*(pullbackComp⁻¹)`.
286	
287	**iter-271 breakthrough — the kernel `whnf` wall is now BYPASSED.** The earlier
288	blocker was a kernel `whnf` blow-up when cancelling `pushPullMap`'s two over-triangle
289	`eqToHom` transports *in situ* (at the concrete `pushforward`/`pullback` comparison
290	objects). The reusable brick `pushPull_transport_cancel` below resolves it: it states
291	the cancellation with the over-triangle equality `h : gl ≫ p₁ = p₂` as a **free
292	hypothesis**, proven by a single `subst h` on *abstract* objects (transports become
293	`eqToHom rfl = 𝟙`, kernel-cheap), and is applied to `pushPullMap` via `erw` — which
294	rewrites the tail without forcing the kernel to unfold the comparison objects. (Plain
295	`rw` does not fire: `SheafOfModules` comps are defeq-not-syntactic, so `erw` is
296	mandatory.) Applying `erw [pushPull_transport_cancel …]` to all three `pushPullMap`
297	occurrences (LHS once, RHS twice) was verified to fire with NO kernel blow-up, leaving
298	both sides transport-light. `pushforwardComp` is `Iso.refl` (strictly functorial), so
299	its comparison factor is `𝟙` and is absorbed by the same `erw`.
300	
301	**Remaining work for `pushPullMap_comp` — a clean post-`subst` pentagon (no kernel
302	issues).** After the three `erw`s, `subst`-ing the two free over-triangles (via a
303	generalised auxiliary lemma with `p₂, p₃` and the over-triangles as free hypotheses,
304	mirroring `pushPull_transport_cancel`) clears the remaining transports except the
305	single associativity cell `eqToHom ((kl≫gl₂)≫p₁ = kl≫(gl₂≫p₁))`. The residual goal is
306	the pure pseudofunctor pentagon: it closes by `pushPull_unit_mate kl gl₂` (to convert
307	the composite unit `η^{kl≫gl₂}`) together with
308	`Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := kl) (g := gl₂) (h := p₁)` — whose four
309	`pullbackComp` 2-cells (`pullbackComp kl (gl₂≫p₁)`, `pullbackComp gl₂ p₁`,
310	`pullbackComp kl gl₂`, `pullbackComp (kl≫gl₂) p₁`) match exactly the four appearing in
311	the goal. The decompositions `pushforward (a≫b) = pushforward a ⋙ pushforward b` and
312	`(pushforward (a≫b)).map φ = (pushforward b).map ((pushforward a).map φ)` both hold by
313	`rfl`. The only friction left is the whiskered-pentagon + `eqToHom` bookkeeping (the
314	standard defeq-not-syntactic `erw` grind), a ~60-100 LOC follow-up; the kernel obstacle
315	that blocked five prior iterations is gone. -/
316	
317	/-- **Base-change unit (mate) identity for the push–pull head.**
318	For composable scheme morphisms `f : A ⟶ B`, `p : B ⟶ Z` and `N : Z.Modules`, the
319	adjunction unit at `N` for `p` followed by the *head* of `pushPullMap` (the
320	pushforward of the unit for `f`, then the pushforward comparison) equals the unit
321	for the composite `f ≫ p` followed by the pushforward of the inverse pullback
322	comparison. This is the mate-calculus core that converts the single-morphism unit
323	`η^{f≫p}` into the iterated units `η^p`, `η^f`; it is the reusable ingredient that
324	the functoriality (pentagon) law of `pushPullMap` repeatedly consumes when
325	splitting a composite unit. Project-local supplement. -/
326	lemma pushPull_unit_mate {A B Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : A ⟶ B) (p : B ⟶ Z)
327	    (N : Z.Modules) :
328	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).unit.app N ≫
329	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map
330	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).unit.app
331	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) ≫
332	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp f p).hom.app
333	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) =
334	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ p)).unit.app N ≫
335	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (f ≫ p)).map
336	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f p).inv.app N) := by
337	  have key := unit_conjugateEquiv
338	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).comp
339	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
340	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ p))
341	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f p).inv N
342	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv, Adjunction.comp_unit_app] at key
343	  simpa only [Category.assoc] using key
344	
345	/-- **Over-triangle transport cancellation for the push–pull tail** (kernel-cheap
346	generalised form). The morphism map `pushPullMap` glues its pullback-comparison leg
347	to the target object `pushPullObj F Y₂` by two `eqToHom` coercions along the
348	over-triangle `g.left ≫ Y₁.hom = Y₂.hom`. Cancelling those coercions *in situ*
349	(at the concrete pushforward/pullback objects) provokes a kernel `whnf` blow-up.
350	This lemma states the cancellation **with the over-triangle equality as a free
351	hypothesis** `h : gl ≫ p₁ = p₂`, so the proof is a single `subst h` (after which
352	the transports become `eqToHom rfl = 𝟙` and vanish — kernel-cheap) followed by
353	`simp`. Applying it to `pushPullMap` via `rw` rewrites the tail without forcing the
354	kernel to unfold the comparison objects: the over-triangle leg
355	`eqToHom ≫ (pushforward p₂).map (pullbackComp).hom ≫ eqToHom` collapses to the
356	transport-light `(pushforward (gl ≫ p₁)).map (pullbackComp).hom ≫ eqToHom`, the
357	single residual `eqToHom` carrying the unavoidable object identification of the
358	codomain `pushPullObj F Y₂`. Reusable pre-coherence brick for `pushPullMap_comp`. -/
359	lemma pushPull_transport_cancel {Y₁ Y₂ : Scheme.{u}}
360	    (gl : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) (p₁ : Y₁ ⟶ X) (p₂ : Y₂ ⟶ X)
361	    (h : gl ≫ p₁ = p₂) (F : X.Modules) :
362	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
363	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback gl).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F))) h) ≫
364	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp gl p₁).hom.app F) ≫
365	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).obj
366	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) h) =
367	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (gl ≫ p₁)).map
368	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp gl p₁).hom.app F) ≫
369	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
370	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) h) := by
371	  subst h
372	  simp
373	
374	/-- **Composite-unit decomposition for the push–pull head.** The adjunction unit
375	`η^{f≫p}` for a composite morphism, expressed through the iterated units `η^p`,
376	`η^f` and the pushforward/pullback comparison isomorphisms. This is the
377	`pushPull_unit_mate` identity solved for `η^{f≫p}` (post-composing with
378	`(f≫p)_*(pullbackComp).hom` cancels the `pullbackComp.inv` factor). Reusable brick
379	for the composition law of `pushPullMap`. -/
380	lemma pushPull_unit_comp {A B Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : A ⟶ B) (p : B ⟶ Z)
381	    (N : Z.Modules) :
382	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ p)).unit.app N =
383	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).unit.app N ≫
384	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map
385	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).unit.app
386	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) ≫
387	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp f p).hom.app
388	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) ≫
389	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (f ≫ p)).map
390	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f p).hom.app N) := by
391	  have m := pushPull_unit_mate f p N
392	  erw [reassoc_of% m, ← Functor.map_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id_app,
393	    CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.comp_id]
394	
395	/-- The pushforward pseudofunctor is *strict* on sheaves of modules: the
396	`pushforwardComp` comparison `2`-cell is the identity on the nose. Holds by `rfl`
397	(`pushforward (a ≫ p) = pushforward a ⋙ pushforward p` definitionally). Project-local
398	collapse used to discharge the pushforward legs of the push–pull pentagon. -/
399	lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_id {Z₁ Z₂ Z₃ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (p : Z₁ ⟶ Z₃)
400	    (M : Z₂.Modules) : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a p).hom.app M = 𝟙 _ :=
401	  rfl
402	
403	/-- **Scheme-level push–pull comparison map with the over-triangle as a free
404	hypothesis.** This is the body of `pushPullMap` with the underlying scheme map
405	`a`, the two structure maps `p₁ p₂`, and the over-triangle `w : a ≫ p₁ = p₂`
406	generalised away from the `Over X` packaging. Spelling the over-triangle as a free
407	hypothesis is what makes the composition law provable by `subst` (after which the
408	`eqToHom` over-triangle transports become `eqToHom rfl = 𝟙` and vanish, dodging the
409	kernel `whnf` wall). By construction `pushPullMap F g = rawPushPullMap g.left Y₁.hom
410	Y₂.hom (Over.w g) F` definitionally. Project-local. -/
411	noncomputable def rawPushPullMap {Z₁ Z₂ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁)
412	    (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X) (p₂ : Z₂ ⟶ X) (w : a ≫ p₁ = p₂) (F : X.Modules) :
413	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ⟶
414	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₂).obj F) :=
415	  (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map
416	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
417	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F)) ≫
418	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a p₁).hom.app
419	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F)) ≫
420	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
421	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F))) w) ≫
422	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
423	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).obj
424	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) w)

427	/-- `pushPullMap` is the `Over X`-instance of `rawPushPullMap`. Holds by `rfl`. -/
428	lemma pushPullMap_eq_raw (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) :
429	    pushPullMap F g = rawPushPullMap g.left Y₁.hom Y₂.hom (Over.w g) F :=
430	  rfl

432	/- **Composition law `pushPullMap_comp` — reduced to an explicit clean pentagon, not
433	yet closed.** The kernel `whnf` wall that blocked iters 264/265/271 is fully removed:
434	the route is `pushPullMap F g = rawPushPullMap g.left Y₁.hom Y₂.hom (Over.w g) F`
435	(`pushPullMap_eq_raw`, by `rfl`), reducing `pushPullMap_comp` to
436	`rawPushPullMap_comp`, which has the two over-triangles `wg : a ≫ p₁ = p₂`,
437	`wh : b ≫ p₂ = p₃` as **free hypotheses**. `subst wg; subst wh` then makes every
438	`eqToHom` over-triangle transport `eqToHom rfl = 𝟙` and vanish (kernel-cheap — no
439	`whnf` blow-up). After `simp only [rawPushPullMap, eqToHom_refl]`,
440	`rw [pushPull_unit_comp b a _]` (the composite-unit decomposition),
441	`simp only [pushforwardComp_hom_app_id]` (pushforward is *strict*: the comparison
442	2-cells are `𝟙` by `rfl`) and absorbing the resulting defeq-`𝟙`s by
443	`repeat erw [Category.id_comp]`, the goal becomes the genuine **pullback
444	pseudofunctor pentagon** (no transports, no `pushforwardComp`):
445	
446	  `(pushforward p₁).map (a_*(η^b) ≫ (b≫a)_*(pullbackComp b a).hom.app(p₁^*F)) ≫`
447	  `  (pushforward (b≫a≫p₁)).map ((pullbackComp (b≫a) p₁).hom.app F) ≫ eqToHom`
448	  ` = (pushforward (a≫p₁)).map (η^b ≫ (pullback b ⋙ pushforward b).map((pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F)) ≫`
449	  `  (pushforward (b≫a≫p₁)).map ((pullbackComp b (a≫p₁)).hom.app F)`
450	
451	where the leading `(pushforward p₁).map (η^a)` has already been peeled and the `η^b`
452	units aligned by `(pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.naturality`. The residual
453	content is `Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := b) (g := a) (h := p₁)`
454	(its four `pullbackComp` 2-cells `pullbackComp b a`, `pullbackComp (b≫a) p₁`,
455	`pullbackComp a p₁`, `pullbackComp b (a≫p₁)` match exactly) together with the single
456	associativity cell `eqToHom ((b≫a)≫p₁ = b≫(a≫p₁))`.
457	
458	**Why it is not yet closed (next-prover dead-ends, all hit this iter):** the regime is
459	pervasively *defeq-not-syntactic*. `rw`/`reassoc_of%` of `Category.assoc`,
460	`Functor.map_comp`, the lead `rfl`-bridge, and `pseudofunctor_associativity` all fail
461	to match visually-identical terms (the pushforward/`SheafOfModules` `≫` and `.map`
462	carry non-syntactic instances). `erw` matches but **whnf-unfolds `pullbackComp` into
463	its raw `TwoSquare.equivNatTrans`/`mateEquiv` mate definition**, exploding the goal.
464	`congr 1` on the defeq-but-not-syntactic leading factors recurses into the functor
465	structure and likewise unfolds `pullbackComp` into HEq goals. The promising next route
466	is to reformulate via the adjunction transpose (`(pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _).homEquiv`)
467	so the pentagon becomes a `pullback`-side identity provable by `pseudofunctor_associativity`
468	without touching the strict-pushforward defeq wall, OR to add a strictness-aware
469	`@[simp]` normal form for `pushforward (a≫b)` so the lead peel is syntactic. The bricks
470	`rawPushPullMap`, `pushPullMap_eq_raw`, `pushPull_unit_comp`, `pushforwardComp_hom_app_id`
471	below are the axiom-clean scaffolding for that final step. -/

473	/-- Clean (transport-free) form of `rawPushPullMap` when the over-triangle is `rfl`
474	(`p₂ = a ≫ p₁`): the pushforward comparison `pushforwardComp` and the `eqToHom`
475	coercions all collapse, leaving `(pushforward p₁).map` of the mate head
476	`η^a ≫ a_*(pullbackComp a p₁).hom`. Project-local helper for `pushPullMap_comp`. -/
477	lemma rawPushPullMap_self {Z₁ Z₂ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X)
478	    (F : X.Modules) :
479	    rawPushPullMap a p₁ (a ≫ p₁) rfl F =
480	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map
481	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
482	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
483	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
484	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F)) := by
485	  simp only [rawPushPullMap, pushforwardComp_hom_app_id, eqToHom_refl, Category.id_comp,
486	    Category.comp_id, Functor.map_comp]
487	  rfl

490	/-- Clean form of `rawPushPullMap` for a general over-triangle `w : a ≫ p₁ = p₂`:
491	the transport-free head `(pushforward p₁).map (η^a ≫ a_*(pullbackComp a p₁).hom)`
492	followed by the single `eqToHom` identifying the target along `w`. Project-local

494	lemma rawPushPullMap_self_gen {Z₁ Z₂ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X)
495	    (p₂ : Z₂ ⟶ X) (w : a ≫ p₁ = p₂) (F : X.Modules) :
496	    rawPushPullMap a p₁ p₂ w F =
497	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map
498	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
499	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
500	            (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
501	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F)) ≫
502	        eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
503	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) w) := by
504	  subst w
505	  rw [rawPushPullMap_self]
506	  exact (Category.comp_id _).symm

508	/-- The pure pullback **pentagon** at `F`, in transport-light form: the content of
509	`pushPullMap_comp` once the units and pushforwards are peeled. It is exactly
510	`Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := b) (g := a) (h := p₁)` evaluated at
511	`F`, with the associator (an identity on components) absorbed and the two leading
512	comparison isos inverted. Project-local. -/
513	lemma pushPull_pentagon {Z₁ Z₂ Z₃ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (b : Z₃ ⟶ Z₂)
514	    (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X) (F : X.Modules) :
515	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
516	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F ≫
517	        eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)
518	          (Category.assoc b a p₁)) =
519	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
520	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F := by
521	  have H := Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := b) (g := a) (h := p₁)
522	  have HF := congrArg (fun t => NatTrans.app t F) H
523	  simp only [NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app,
524	    Functor.associator_hom_app, eqToHom_app, Category.assoc, Functor.comp_obj,
525	    Category.id_comp] at HF
526	  -- HF : A1⁻¹ ≫ B1⁻¹ ≫ C ≫ D = eqToHom eF, with the associator (= 𝟙) absorbed.
527	  -- Cancel the two leading isos `B1 = (pullback b).map (pullbackComp a p₁).hom`, `A1 = pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)`
528	  -- against their inverses (no `IsIso` instances needed: `Iso.hom_inv_id_app` + functoriality),
529	  -- then feed `HF` in via `congrArg`/`trans` (defeq, so no fragile `rw [HF]` matching).
530	  have cancel : (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
531	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F ≫
532	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).inv.app F ≫
533	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).inv.app F) ≫
534	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
535	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F =
536	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
537	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F := by
538	    have h1 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F ≫
539	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).inv.app F = 𝟙 _ := Iso.hom_inv_id_app _ _
540	    have h2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
541	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).inv.app F) = 𝟙 _ :=
542	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map_comp _ _).symm.trans
543	        ((congrArg (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map (Iso.hom_inv_id_app _ _)).trans
544	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map_id _))

642	/-- **Push–pull functor `G` — composition law** (contravariant functoriality).
643	For composable morphisms `g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁`, `h : Y₃ ⟶ Y₂` of `X`-schemes,
644	`G(h ≫ g) = G(g) ≫ G(h)` (writing `≫` for `Over X`-composition; in the informal
645	`∘`-notation of the blueprint this is `G(g ∘ h) = G(h) ∘ G(g)`). Together with
646	`pushPullMap_id` this assembles `pushPullObj`/`pushPullMap` into a genuine functor
647	`(Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`. Project-local (`lem:push_pull_comp`). -/
648	lemma pushPullMap_comp (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ Y₃ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) (h : Y₃ ⟶ Y₂) :
649	    pushPullMap F (h ≫ g) = pushPullMap F g ≫ pushPullMap F h := by
650	  rw [pushPullMap_eq_raw, pushPullMap_eq_raw, pushPullMap_eq_raw]
651	  exact rawPushPullMap_comp g.left h.left Y₁.hom Y₂.hom Y₃.hom (Over.w g) (Over.w h) F
652	
653	/-- **Relative {\v C}ech complex from a cosimplicial nerve** (coherence-free
654	plumbing). Given `f : X ⟶ S` and an augmented cosimplicial object `N` of
655	`O_X`-modules, produce the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` by:
656	forgetting the augmentation (`CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop`), pushing the
657	cosimplicial object forward along `f` (`CosimplicialObject.whiskering` applied to
658	`Scheme.Modules.pushforward f`), and taking the alternating coface-map cochain
659	complex (`alternatingCofaceMapComplex`). This is the entire passage `CechNerve ↦
660	CechComplex`, and it uses no `pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` coherence — only
661	the (pre)additivity of `S.Modules`. Project-local. -/
662	noncomputable def relativeCechComplexOfNerve (f : X ⟶ S)
663	    (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules) : CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
664	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex S.Modules).obj
665	    (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering X.Modules S.Modules).obj
666	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)).obj (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N))
667	
668	/-- **Relative Čech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf** (Stacks, Cohomology of
669	Schemes, `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`).
670	
671	For `f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X` (with all intersections
672	affine, e.g. `X` separated) and a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`, the *relative Čech
673	complex* `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the cochain complex of `O_S`-modules with degree-`p` term
674	```
675	  Čᵖ(𝒰, F) = ∏_{(i₀,…,i_p)} (f|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}}),
676	```
677	and differential the alternating sum of the restriction maps
678	`(d s)_{i₀…i_{p+1}} = Σⱼ (-1)ʲ s_{i₀…î_j…i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i₀…i_{p+1}}}`. Over an
679	affine `U = Spec A` with `F|_U = M~` and a standard cover by the `D(fᵢ)`, this is
680	the complex of localisations `∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}f_{i₁}} → ⋯`. Each term
681	is quasi-coherent because the intersections are affine and the pushforward of a
682	quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated morphism is
683	quasi-coherent.
684	
685	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
686	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
687	noncomputable def CechComplex (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
688	    CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
689	  -- Construction (Stacks): apply the relative pushforward `f_*` over each finite
690	  -- intersection to the Čech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F`, then take the alternating-sum
691	  -- Čech differential. This is exactly the coherence-free plumbing
692	  -- `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`, so `CechComplex` is genuinely *defined* in terms
693	  -- of the nerve: an axiom-clean `CechNerve` immediately yields an axiom-clean
694	  -- `CechComplex`. The only remaining hole is `CechNerve` itself.
695	  relativeCechComplexOfNerve f (CechNerve 𝒰 F)
696	
697	/-- **Čech acyclicity on affines** (Stacks 02KG;
698	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial` and
699	`lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero`).
700	
701	Let `X = Spec A` be affine, `F` a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module, and `𝒰` a finite
702	standard open cover (the `fᵢ ∈ A` generate the unit ideal). Then the relative
703	Čech complex (here with `f` an affine morphism) has vanishing cohomology in all
704	positive degrees: `Hᵖ = 0` for `p ≥ 1`, equivalently `Hᵖ(X, F) = 0` for `p > 0`
705	(Serre vanishing for quasi-coherent sheaves on affines).
706	
707	The proof (Stacks): write `F|_X = M~`; the Čech complex of the standard cover is
708	the complex of localisations, and `Hᵖ = 0` for `p > 0` is equivalent to exactness
709	of the extended complex `0 → M → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ⋯`. Exactness is checked after
710	localising at an arbitrary prime `𝔭`; choosing `i_fix` with `f_{i_fix} ∉ 𝔭`, the
711	prescription `h(s)_{i₀…i_p} = s_{i_fix i₀…i_p}` is a contracting homotopy, so the
712	localised complex is exact, hence so is the complex. The Čech-to-cohomology
713	comparison on the basis of affine opens then gives the sheaf statement. -/
714	theorem CechAcyclic.affine [IsAffine X] (f : X ⟶ S) [IsAffineHom f]
715	    (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
716	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (p : ℕ) (hp : 1 ≤ p) :
717	    IsZero ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology p) := by
718	  -- Proof (Stacks 02KG): on the affine `X = Spec A` the Čech complex of the
719	  -- standard cover is the complex of localisations; positive-degree exactness
720	  -- follows from the prime-local contracting homotopy `h(s)_{i₀…i_p} =
721	  -- s_{i_fix i₀…i_p}` (where `f_{i_fix} ∉ 𝔭`), giving `(dh + hd) = id`. Needs the
722	  -- explicit localisation description of `CechComplex` on affines and the
723	  -- module-level homotopy, currently absent from Mathlib for `Scheme.Modules`.
724	  sorry
725	
726	/-- **The Čech complex computes the higher direct images** (Stacks 02KE;
727	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent` and
728	`lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application`).
729	
730	Let `f : X ⟶ S` be separated and quasi-compact, `F` a quasi-coherent
731	`O_X`-module, and `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X` (so, by separatedness,
732	every intersection is affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative Čech
733	complex compute the higher direct images: for every `i ≥ 0` there is a canonical
734	isomorphism of `O_S`-modules
735	```
736	  Hⁱ(Č•(𝒰, F)) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F.
737	```
738	In particular, over an affine base `S = Spec A`, taking global sections gives
739	`Hⁱ(X, F) = Čⁱ(𝒰, F) = H⁰(S, Rⁱ f_* F)` as `A`-modules.
740	
741	We state the isomorphism as `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` and compare against the
742	derived-functor higher direct image `higherDirectImage` wherever the latter is
743	defined (`HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules`).
744	
745	The proof (Stacks 02KE): the question is local on `S`, reducing to `S` affine; by
746	affine acyclicity (`CechAcyclic.affine`) the higher cohomology of `F` over each
747	affine intersection vanishes, so the Čech-to-cohomology spectral sequence
748	collapses to its `q = 0` row, identifying Čech cohomology with sheaf cohomology;
749	over affine `S` the Leray spectral sequence then degenerates (Serre vanishing for
750	the quasi-coherent `Rⁱ f_* F`), giving the stated isomorphism. -/
751	theorem cech_computes_higherDirectImage [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]
752	    (f : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact f] [IsSeparated f] (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
753	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (i : ℕ) :
754	    Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F) := by
755	  -- Proof (Stacks 02KE): reduce to `S` affine; affine acyclicity
756	  -- (`CechAcyclic.affine`) collapses the Čech-to-cohomology spectral sequence to
757	  -- its `q = 0` row, and the Leray spectral sequence degenerates by Serre
758	  -- vanishing for the quasi-coherent `Rⁱ f_* F`, yielding the comparison iso.
759	  -- Needs the two spectral sequences for `Scheme.Modules`, currently absent from
760	  -- Mathlib.
761	  sorry
762	
763	end AlgebraicGeometry

{"symbol":"/-- The augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an open cover `𝒰`, as an augmented

has all finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks used by

input.new_string:
map_id Y := pushPullMap_id F Y.unop
  map_comp φ ψ := pushPullMap_comp F φ.unop ψ.unop

/-- The geometric Čech backbone, lifted to a **simplicial object in `Over X`**: each
fibre power carries its structure map to `X` (the augmentation of `coverCechNerve`),
and the face/degeneracy maps are `X`-morphisms by naturality of the augmentation into
the constant functor. Project-local; obtained from `coverCechNerve` by
`CategoryTheory.Over.lift` applied to the augmentation natural transformation. -/
noncomputable def coverCechNerveOver (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) : SimplicialObject (Over X) :=
  Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom

/-- The cosimplicial `O_X`-module obtained by post-composing the over-category Čech
backbone `coverCechNerveOver` (read contravariantly, via `Functor.leftOp`) with the
push–pull functor `pushPullFunctor F = G`. This is the underlying cosimplicial object
of `CechNerve` (before adjoining the augmentation `F`). Project-local. -/
noncomputable def cechNerveCosimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
    CosimplicialObject X.Modules :=
  (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).leftOp ⋙ pushPullFunctor F

/-- The over-category Čech backbone as an **augmented** simplicial object in `Over X`:
`coverCechNerveOver` augmented by the terminal object `Over.mk (𝟙 X)` of `Over X`. The
augmentation map at each simplicial level is the unique morphism to the terminal object
(its underlying scheme map is the level's structure map to `X`), and the augmentation
coherence condition holds automatically because the augmentation target is terminal.
Project-local. -/
noncomputable def coverCechNerveOverAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
    SimplicialObject.Augmented (Over X) :=
  SimplicialObject.augment (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰) (Over.mk (𝟙 X))
    (Over.mkIdTerminal.from _)
    (fun _ _ _ => Over.mkIdTerminal.hom_ext _ _)

input.new_string:
(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰 : SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ ⥤ Over X).rightOp ⋙ pushPullFunctor F

/-- **Čech nerve of an affine open cover** (Stacks, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech
cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves).

For a scheme `X`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰 : X = ⋃ Uᵢ` and a quasi-coherent
sheaf `F`, the *Čech nerve* is the augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules whose
object in simplicial degree `p` is the product, over the `(p+1)`-tuples `(i₀,…,i_p)` of
indices, of the direct images `(j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})` of the restriction of
`F` to the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{i₀…i_p} = U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{i_p}` along the open
immersion `j_{i₀…i_p} : U_{i₀…i_p} ↪ X`. Faces are the restriction maps that omit one
index, degeneracies repeat one index, and the augmentation in degree `-1` is `F` itself
on all of `X`. When `X` is separated each intersection `U_{i₀…i_p}` is affine.

This is now *constructed*, not postulated: the geometric backbone `coverCechNerveOverAug`
(the augmented Čech nerve of the cover as an augmented simplicial object in `Over X`,
unconditional because `Scheme` has all finite limits) is read contravariantly
(`SimplicialObject.Augmented.rightOp`) and whiskered by the push–pull functor
`pushPullFunctor F = G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. The whiskering
transports both the cosimplicial structure and the augmentation, so the augmentation
point is `G` applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`, namely
`(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F ≅ F`. The functor laws of `G` (`pushPullMap_id`, `pushPullMap_comp`)
are what make this assembly legitimate.

Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
noncomputable def CechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
    CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules :=
  (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj (Over X)ᵒᵖ X.Modules (pushPullFunctor F)).obj
    (coverCechNerveOverAug 𝒰).rightOp

/-- **Relative {\v C}ech complex from a cosimplicial nerve** (coherence-free

1	\chapter{{\v C}ech computation of higher direct images $R^i f_*$ (unconditional)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean
4	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
5	% quasi-coherent sheaves and \S Quasi-coherence of higher direct images
6	% (Lemmas \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial},
7	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero},
8	% \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent},
9	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}) (read from
10	% references/stacks-coherent.tex).
11	
12	\section{Setup and motivation}
13	
14	This chapter constructs the higher derived direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) for
15	\(i \geq 1\) \emph{without appealing to injective resolutions in the category of
16	sheaves of modules}. The companion development of
17	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage} defines \(R^i f_*\) as a right
18	derived functor, which in a formalisation requires the ambient category of
19	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to have enough injectives; that property is not currently
20	available for sheaves of modules over a sheaf of rings whose value category varies
21	over the site. The {\v C}ech approach developed here sidesteps the issue entirely:
22	it computes \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as the cohomology of an explicit complex built
23	from the pushforwards of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the finite intersections of an affine
24	open cover, and so produces an \emph{unconditional} construction of \(R^i f_*\) for
25	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) and separated quasi-compact \(f\).
26	
27	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
28	separated (so that all finite intersections of an affine open cover of \(X\) are
29	again affine), and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. We
30	write \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) for the category of quasi-coherent
31	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. A base change of \(f\) along a morphism \(g : S' \to S\)
32	is recorded by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
33	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
34	\[
35	  \begin{array}{ccc}
36	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
37	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
38	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
39	  \end{array}
40	\]
41	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), \(g' : X' \to X\) is the first
42	projection, and \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the pullback of
43	\(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
44	
45	\section{The {\v C}ech nerve and {\v C}ech complex of an affine cover}
46	
47	\begin{definition}
48	
49	\leanok
50	[{\v C}ech nerve of an affine open cover]
51	  \label{def:cech_nerve}
52	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve}
53	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, lem:push_pull_id, lem:push_pull_comp}
54	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
55	  % quasi-coherent sheaves, opening paragraph and Lemma
56	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}
57	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L36--67).
58	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
59	  % Let $U \subset X$ be an affine open.
60	  % Recall that a {\it standard open covering} of $U$ is a covering
61	  % of the form $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$
62	  % where $f_1, \ldots, f_n \in \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)$ generate
63	  % the unit ideal, see
64	  % Schemes, Definition \ref{schemes-definition-standard-covering}."
65	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
66	  quasi-coherent sheaves.}
67	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
68	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite open cover of \(X\) by
69	  affine opens, with index set \(I\). For a finite nonempty tuple
70	  \(i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I\) write
71	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = U_{i_0} \cap \cdots \cap U_{i_p}\) for the corresponding
72	  intersection. The \emph{{\v C}ech nerve} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the augmented
73	  \emph{cosimplicial} object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) whose object in
74	  cosimplicial degree \(p\) is the direct image, along the open immersions
75	  \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\), of the restriction
76	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (or of any chosen quasi-coherent sheaf) to the
77	  \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,
78	  \[
79	    [p] \;\longmapsto\;
80	      \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
81	        (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_* \,\bigl(\,\cdot\,|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
82	  \]
83	  with cofaces given by the restriction maps that omit one index and
84	  codegeneracies by the maps that repeat one index; the augmentation in degree
85	  \(-1\) is the object on all of \(X\). The variance is essential: the geometric
86	  nerve of the cover (the iterated fibre powers of \(\coprod_i U_i\) over \(X\),
87	  introduced in \S\ref{sec:cech_three_part} below) is a \emph{simplicial} object
88	  in \emph{schemes}, but applying the covariant direct-image functor turns it into
89	  a \emph{cosimplicial} object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\); consequently the
90	  associated alternating-coface complex is a \emph{cochain} complex (differentials
91	  raising degree), not a chain complex. When \(X\) is separated each intersection
92	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is itself affine. The local affine model is the
93	  \emph{standard open covering} \(U = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) of an affine open
94	  \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) by basic opens, where \(f_1, \ldots, f_n \in A\)
95	  generate the unit ideal; on such a cover the intersections are again basic opens
96	  \(D(f_{i_0} \cdots f_{i_p})\).
97	\end{definition}
98	
99	\begin{definition}
100	
101	\leanok
102	[{\v C}ech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf]
103	  \label{def:cech_complex}
104	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex}
105	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, def:relative_cech_complex_of_nerve}
106	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
107	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof)
108	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L59--77).
109	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
110	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
111	  % is identified with the complex
112	  % $$
113	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
114	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
115	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
116	  % \ldots
117	  % $$
118	  % We are asked to show that the extended complex
119	  % \begin{equation}
120	  % \label{equation-extended}
121	  % 0 \to
122	  % M \to
123	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
124	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
125	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
126	  % \ldots
127	  % \end{equation}
128	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
129	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact."
130	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
131	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover {\v C}ech
132	  vanishing).}
133	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
134	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with
135	  all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) affine (e.g.\ \(X\) separated). The
136	  \emph{{\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
137	  is the cochain complex obtained by applying the global-sections functor to the
138	  {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} evaluated at \(\mathcal{F}\);
139	  in degree \(p\) it is
140	  \[
141	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
142	      = \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
143	        \mathcal{F}\bigl(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
144	  \]
145	  with differential \(d : \check{\mathcal{C}}^p \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^{p+1}\) the
146	  alternating sum \((d s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\,
147	  s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}\) of the
148	  restriction maps. Over an affine \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) with
149	  \(\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}\) and a standard cover by the
150	  \(D(f_i)\), this is the complex
151	  \(\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to \cdots\) of
152	  localisations. The \emph{relative {\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet
153	  (\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) for \(f : X \to S\) is the analogous complex in
154	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) obtained by applying \(f_*\) over each finite
155	  intersection instead of global sections; its terms are
156	  \(\prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} (f|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})_*
157	  \bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr)\), and it is a complex in
158	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) because each \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine and the
159	  pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated
160	  morphism is quasi-coherent.
161	\end{definition}
162	
163	\subsection{The three-part construction of the relative {\v C}ech complex}
164	\label{sec:cech_three_part}
165	
166	It is convenient to factor the construction of
167	Definitions~\ref{def:cech_nerve}--\ref{def:cech_complex} into three independent
168	pieces. Two of them are purely formal and unconditional; the entire mathematical
169	content that is not yet available off the shelf is isolated in the middle piece.
170	
171	\paragraph{(1) Geometric backbone — the augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow.}
172	Package the affine cover as a single morphism
173	\(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) (the canonical map out of the
174	coproduct induced by the inclusions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\)). The augmented
175	{\v C}ech nerve of this arrow is an augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
176	\(X\): its object in simplicial degree \(p\) is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
177	\[
178	  \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
179	    \;=\;
180	    \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
181	      U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
182	\]
183	with the augmentation the cover map to \(X\). Because the \(U_i\) are open
184	subschemes of \(X\), each fibre product \(U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p}\)
185	is canonically the intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\). This backbone exists
186	\emph{unconditionally}: it uses only that the category of schemes has coproducts
187	and finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the {\v C}ech nerve of an
188	arrow). It carries no sheaf-theoretic data yet — it is the simplicial diagram of
189	spaces over which the construction is indexed.
190	
191	\paragraph{(2) Push--pull functor — lifting the backbone to modules.}
192	The geometric backbone is turned into a cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module by
193	the \emph{relative-direct-image functor on the over-category}
194	\[
195	  G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
196	  \qquad
197	  (Y \xrightarrow{\,p\,} X) \;\longmapsto\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
198	\]
199	sending an \(X\)-scheme \(p : Y \to X\) to the pushforward along \(p\) of the
200	pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\). Composing \(G\) with the (opposite of the) simplicial
201	backbone of (1) produces exactly the augmented cosimplicial object of
202	Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: on the degree-\(p\) piece
203	\(p : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\) one has
204	\(p_* p^*\mathcal{F} = (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\),
205	and a morphism of \(X\)-schemes \(h : (Y_1, p_1) \to (Y_2, p_2)\) (so
206	\(h \circ p_2 = p_1\)\,, reading \(p\) as the structure map) induces the
207	restriction comparison \(G(p_2) \to G(p_1)\) via the unit/counit of the
208	pull-push adjunction together with the over-triangle identifying the two
209	structure maps. This functor \(G\) is the \emph{one non-formal step} in the whole
210	construction. Its functoriality --- that \(G\) respects identities and composition
211	of morphisms over \(X\) --- is precisely a statement about the coherence of the
212	comparison isomorphisms \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) and
213	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\) for pushforward and pullback of sheaves of
214	modules, together with their compatibility with the adjunction units. This
215	functoriality is \emph{not} coupled to the project's tensor--pullback substrate;
216	it consumes only Mathlib's pseudofunctor packaging of pullback and pushforward.
217	Concretely, pullback and pushforward of sheaves of modules are organised as a
218	pseudofunctor \(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to
219	\mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\) (sending a morphism to the adjoint pair
220	\(p^* \dashv p_*\)), and the comparison isomorphisms
221	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\), \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) are its structure
222	\(2\)-cells. The functoriality of \(G\) needs exactly three facts about this
223	packaging: the mate identity expressing the conjugate of the inverse pullback
224	comparison as the pushforward comparison (the lemma
225	\texttt{conjugateEquiv\_pullbackComp\_inv}), its identity normalisation
226	\texttt{conjugateEquiv\_pullbackId\_hom}, and the pseudofunctor unitor and pentagon
227	(associativity) coherences \texttt{pseudofunctor\_left\_unitality},
228	\texttt{pseudofunctor\_right\_unitality}, \texttt{pseudofunctor\_associativity}.
229	All of these are already available off the shelf, so the functoriality of the
230	relative-direct-image functor is a \emph{consumer of Mathlib's coherence},
231	independent of the project-local sheafification/pullback machinery of the
232	tensor--pullback substrate. The laws are nonetheless not formal trivialities: each
233	is a multi-step mate calculation that transports the comparison isomorphisms along
234	the over-triangle \(g \circ p_2 = p_1\) of structure maps and past the adjunction
235	unit, gluing them across two \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) coercions; the obstacle is the
236	bookkeeping of those transports, not a missing geometric ingredient.
237	
238	\paragraph{(3) Coherence-free plumbing — from the nerve to the cochain complex.}
239	The passage from the augmented cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module of (1)--(2)
240	to the relative {\v C}ech complex in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) is again purely
241	formal and uses \emph{no} pushforward/pullback coherence. One forgets the
242	augmentation, pushes the cosimplicial object forward along \(f : X \to S\) (a
243	covariant operation applied degreewise), and takes the alternating-coface-map
244	cochain complex of the resulting cosimplicial object of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules.
245	This step relies only on the preadditivity of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\): the
246	alternating sum \(\sum_{j} (-1)^j d^j\) of the cofaces is defined in any
247	preadditive category and squares to zero by the cosimplicial identities. Thus the
248	relative {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
249	of Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} is genuinely \emph{defined} in terms of the
250	{\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: once the nerve is constructed,
251	the complex follows with no further input. In summary, of the three pieces only
252	the push--pull functor (2) requires content beyond formal category theory, and
253	that content is the pushforward/pullback pseudofunctor coherence supplied by
254	Mathlib (the comparison \(2\)-cells of the pseudofunctor
255	\(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to \mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\)
256	and their unitor/pentagon identities), not any project-local ingredient.
257	
258	\paragraph{Formal bricks of the three-part construction.}
259	The geometric backbone (1), the push--pull functor (2), and the plumbing (3) are
260	recorded as the following declarations. The backbone and plumbing are
261	unconditional; the push--pull object and morphism maps are likewise unconditional
262	bricks, and the functor laws assembling them are the lone coherence statement.
263	
264	\begin{definition}\leanok
265	[Arrow of an affine cover]
266	  \label{def:cover_arrow}
267	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrow}
268	  Let \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be an affine open cover. Its
269	  \emph{cover arrow} is the single morphism
270	  \(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) obtained as the universal map out of
271	  the coproduct induced by the open immersions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\). Packaging
272	  the cover as one arrow lets the generic {\v C}ech-nerve-of-an-arrow machinery
273	  apply.
274	\end{definition}
275	
276	\begin{definition}\leanok
277	[Augmented {\v C}ech nerve of a cover]
278	  \label{def:cover_cech_nerve}
279	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerve}
280	  \uses{def:cover_arrow}
281	  The augmented {\v C}ech nerve of the cover arrow of
282	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_arrow}: the augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
283	  \(X\) whose degree-\(p\) object is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
284	  \[
285	    \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
286	      = \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
287	        U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
288	  \]
289	  with augmentation the cover map to \(X\). It exists unconditionally because the
290	  category of schemes has all finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the
291	  {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow). This is the geometric backbone (1).
292	\end{definition}
293	
294	\begin{definition}\leanok
295	[Push--pull object map]
296	  \label{def:push_pull_obj}
297	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullObj}
298	  The object map of the push--pull functor \(G\): to an \(X\)-scheme
299	  \(p : Y \to X\) it assigns
300	  \[
301	    G(Y, p) \;=\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
302	  \]
303	  the pushforward along \(p\) of the pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\).
304	\end{definition}
305	
306	\begin{definition}\leanok
307	[Push--pull comparison map]
308	  \label{def:push_pull_map}
309	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap}
310	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj}
311	  The morphism map of \(G\). A morphism \(g : (Y_2, p_2) \to (Y_1, p_1)\) of
312	  \(X\)-schemes has underlying map \(\bar g : Y_2 \to Y_1\) satisfying the
313	  over-triangle \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\); the contravariant functor produces the
314	  restriction comparison \(G(Y_1, p_1) \to G(Y_2, p_2)\) as the five-step composite
315	  \[
316	    p_{1*}\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
317	      \xrightarrow{\,p_{1*}(\eta)\,}
318	    p_{1*}\,\bar g_*\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
319	      \xrightarrow{\,\sim\,}
320	    (p_1 \circ \bar g)_*\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
321	      \;=\;
322	    p_{2*}\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
323	      \xrightarrow{\,p_{2*}(\sim)\,}
324	    p_{2*}\,p_2^*\mathcal{F},
325	  \]
326	  where \(\eta\) is the unit of the adjunction \(\bar g^* \dashv \bar g_*\), the
327	  second arrow is the pushforward comparison \(p_{1*}\bar g_* \cong (p_1 \bar g)_*\),
328	  the equality is the substitution \(p_2 = p_1 \circ \bar g\) of the over-triangle,
329	  and the last arrow applies \(p_{2*}\) to the pullback comparison
330	  \(\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (p_1 \bar g)^* = p_2^*\). The two over-triangle
331	  substitutions are realised as coercions along \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\).
332	\end{definition}
333	
334	\begin{lemma}
335	[Push--pull functor — identity law]
336	  \label{lem:push_pull_id}
337	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_id}
338	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map}
339	  The morphism map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} respects identities:
340	  \(G(\mathrm{id}_Y) = \mathrm{id}_{G(Y)}\) for every \(X\)-scheme \(Y\).
341	\end{lemma}
342	
343	\begin{proof}
344	  A statement purely about the pushforward/pullback comparison isomorphisms, with no
345	  sectionwise content. Rewrite the head \(p_*(\eta)\) followed by the pushforward
346	  comparison as the conjugate-mate of the inverse pullback comparison
347	  (\texttt{conjugateEquiv\_pullbackComp\_inv}); the identity leg then collapses by the
348	  identity normalisation \texttt{conjugateEquiv\_pullbackId\_hom} together with the right
349	  unitality \texttt{pseudofunctor\_right\_unitality}, and the two over-triangle
350	  \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) coercions cancel against the unitality coercion.
351	\end{proof}
352	
353	\begin{lemma}
354	[Push--pull functor — composition law]
355	  \label{lem:push_pull_comp}
356	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_comp}
357	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map, lem:push_pull_unit_mate, lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
358	  The morphism map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} is contravariantly functorial:
359	  for composable morphisms \(h : Y_3 \to Y_2\), \(g : Y_2 \to Y_1\) of \(X\)-schemes,
360	  \[
361	    G(g \circ h) = G(h) \circ G(g).
362	  \]
363	  Together with Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_id} this assembles
364	  Definitions~\ref{def:push_pull_obj}--\ref{def:push_pull_map} into a functor
365	  \(G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \to \operatorname{QCoh}(X)\); composing \(G\) with
366	  the backbone of Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve} yields the {\v C}ech nerve of
367	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}.
368	\end{lemma}
369	
370	\begin{proof}
371	  \uses{lem:push_pull_unit_mate, lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
372	  The composition law of any right-adjoint-valued contravariant assignment is an instance
373	  of \emph{mate-of-composite \(=\) composite-of-mates}. Concretely, the pushforward
374	  (right-adjoint) comparison is the conjugate of the pullback (left-adjoint) comparison, and
375	  the general identity
376	  \[
377	    \mathrm{conjugateEquiv}(\mathrm{adj}_1,\mathrm{adj}_2)\,\alpha \circ
378	    \mathrm{conjugateEquiv}(\mathrm{adj}_2,\mathrm{adj}_3)\,\beta
379	    = \mathrm{conjugateEquiv}(\mathrm{adj}_1,\mathrm{adj}_3)\,(\beta \circ \alpha)
380	  \]
381	  (\texttt{CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv\_comp}, \texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Adjunction/Mates.lean})
382	  is exactly the content of the composition law. Using injectivity of the
383	  \(\mathrm{conjugateEquiv}\) bijection, the claim reduces to the corresponding identity on
384	  the \emph{pullback} side, which is the pseudofunctor pentagon
385	  \texttt{pseudofunctor\_associativity}; the composite adjunction unit is handled by the mate core
386	  Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_unit_mate} (and its solved form
387	  \texttt{pushPull\_unit\_comp}), and the over-triangle \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) transports are
388	  discharged by Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}.
389	\end{proof}
390	
391	\begin{lemma}
392	[Base-change unit (mate) identity for the push--pull head]
393	  \label{lem:push_pull_unit_mate}
394	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_unit_mate}
395	  \uses{def:push_pull_map}
396	  For composable scheme morphisms \(f : A \to B\), \(p : B \to Z\) and a
397	  \(\mathcal{O}_Z\)-module \(N\), the adjunction unit \(\eta^p_N\) of
398	  \(p^* \dashv p_*\) at \(N\), followed by \(p_*\) of the unit \(\eta^f\) for
399	  \(f^* \dashv f_*\) at \(p^*N\), followed by the pushforward comparison
400	  \(p_* f_* \cong (f \circ p)_*\), equals the unit \(\eta^{f \circ p}_N\) for the
401	  composite followed by \((f \circ p)_*\) applied to the inverse pullback
402	  comparison \((f \circ p)^* \cong f^* p^*\):
403	  \[
404	    \eta^p_N \;\circ\; p_*(\eta^f_{p^*N}) \;\circ\;
405	      \bigl(p_* f_* \cong (f \circ p)_*\bigr)
406	    \;=\;
407	    \eta^{f \circ p}_N \;\circ\; (f \circ p)_*\bigl((f\circ p)^* \cong f^* p^*\bigr)^{-1}.
408	  \]
409	  This is the mate-calculus core that converts the single-morphism unit
410	  \(\eta^{f \circ p}\) into the iterated units \(\eta^p, \eta^f\); it is the
411	  reusable ingredient consumed by the functoriality (pentagon) law of the
412	  push--pull comparison map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map}.
413	\end{lemma}
414	
415	\begin{proof}
416	  Proved directly in Lean.
417	\end{proof}
418	
419	\begin{lemma}
420	[Over-triangle transport cancellation for the push--pull tail]
421	  \label{lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
422	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_transport_cancel}
423	  \uses{def:push_pull_map}

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean

## Summary

**P1 primary objective COMPLETE + P2 stretch objective COMPLETE.** Both axiom-clean
(`propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound` only — verified with `lean_verify`).

On entry the file did **not** compile: `pushPullMap_comp` and its whole dependency
chain were already written by a prior iter, but the supporting lemma `pushPull_pentagon`
had a hard `rewrite` error (line 545), so the file was red. Fixing that one error
unblocked the entire push–pull functoriality stack. I then assembled the functor `G`
and, with both functor laws in hand, **constructed `CechNerve`** (the P2 stretch),
eliminating that `sorry` entirely.

File now compiles with only style warnings (long lines / maxHeartbeats comments, all
pre-existing) and the **two genuinely-blocked, out-of-scope sorries** that the
objective told me not to touch: `CechAcyclic.affine` (P3) and
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` (P5, protected).

## pushPull_pentagon (was line ~545) — the blocking error
### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** the prior `cancel` sub-proof used `rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]`.
- **Result:** FAILED — `rewrite` could not find the iso-cancellation pattern.
- **Root cause (defeq-not-syntactic):** `h1`/`h2` stated their objects in
  composed-functor form `(pullback (a≫p₁) ⋙ pullback b).obj F` while the goal carried
  them applied `(pullback b).obj ((pullback (a≫p₁)).obj F)`. Definitionally equal, but
  `rw` needs syntactic agreement of the `≫` implicit object args, so no match.
- **Dead ends confirmed:** `rw [Iso.hom_inv_id_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, …]` —
  same matching wall. The `lean_multi_attempt` line-REPL reported `diagnostics: []`
  (false positive — the documented trap); the *real* file diagnostic showed the error.
  Trust `lean_diagnostic_messages` on the actual file, not the REPL here.
### Attempt 2
- **Approach:** normalise object forms first, then the original cancellation:
  `simp only [Functor.comp_obj] at h1 h2 ⊢` followed by `rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED. `Functor.comp_obj` rewrites `(F ⋙ G).obj` → `G.obj (F.obj _)`
  in `h1`/`h2`/goal, making the `≫` object args syntactically agree so `reassoc_of%`
  fires.
- **Key insight:** for iso cancellation inside a long `SheafOfModules` composite,
  syntactic object-form alignment (`Functor.comp_obj`) is the lever, not stronger
  rewrite tactics.
- Also dropped a now-unused `Category.assoc` from the `HF` `simp only` (lint).

## pushPullMap_comp (line ~648) — P1 PRIMARY OBJECTIVE
- **Result:** RESOLVED (it was already written; closing the `pushPull_pentagon` error
  above made it and its entire cone — `rawPushPullMap_comp`, `pushPull_pentagon`,
  `rawPushPullMap_self(_gen)`, etc. — compile). Verified axiom-clean.
- No change to the protected signature; `pushPullMap_comp` is not itself protected.

## pushPullFunctor (NEW, line ~662) — assembles G
- **Approach:** bundle `pushPullObj`/`pushPullMap` + the two functor laws into
  `(Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`. `map φ := pushPullMap F φ.unop`,
  `map_comp φ ψ := pushPullMap_comp F φ.unop ψ.unop`.
- **Dead end:** initially wrote `pushPullMap_comp F ψ.unop φ.unop` (args swapped) →
  `isDefEq` timeout. Correct order: `g := φ.unop`, `h := ψ.unop` (since in the op
  category `(φ ≫ ψ).unop = ψ.unop ≫ φ.unop` and the law is
  `pushPullMap F (h ≫ g) = pushPullMap F g ≫ pushPullMap F h`).
- **Result:** RESOLVED, axiom-clean. This is the planner's `G`.

## CechNerve (was line 83, now ~726) — P2 STRETCH OBJECTIVE
- **Approach (clean, no hand-built coherence):**
  1. `coverCechNerveOver` — lift the geometric backbone to `SimplicialObject (Over X)`
     via `CategoryTheory.Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom`
     (the augmentation natural transformation supplies the structure maps to `X`).
  2. `coverCechNerveOverAug` — augment it to `SimplicialObject.Augmented (Over X)` via
     `SimplicialObject.augment` with point the **terminal** object `Over.mk (𝟙 X)`
     (`Over.mkIdTerminal`). The augmentation coherence `w` is then *automatic* by
     terminal uniqueness (`Over.mkIdTerminal.hom_ext`) — no manual diagram chase.
  3. `CechNerve` — `(SimplicialObject.Augmented.rightOp …)` flips simplicial→cosimplicial
     into `CosimplicialObject.Augmented (Over X)ᵒᵖ`, then
     `CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj (Over X)ᵒᵖ X.Modules (pushPullFunctor F)`
     transports both the cosimplicial structure **and** the augmentation through `G`.
     The augmentation point becomes `G(⟨X,𝟙 X⟩) = (𝟙 X)_*(𝟙 X)^* F ≅ F`.
- **Why this works now:** the contravariant `pushPullFunctor` (needs both `pushPullMap_id`
  and `pushPullMap_comp`) is exactly the missing ingredient; `rightOp` + `whiskeringObj`
  are off-the-shelf, so the augmentation never has to be hand-proved.
- **Tactic notes / dead ends:**
  - `.leftOp` vs `.rightOp`: Mathlib's `Functor.leftOp : (C ⥤ Dᵒᵖ) → Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D`
    (un-ops the codomain). To turn `SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ ⥤ Over X` into
    `SimplexCategory ⥤ (Over X)ᵒᵖ` use **`.rightOp`**.
  - `SimplicialObject (Over X)` does not reducibly expose its `ᵒᵖ` shape, so `.rightOp`
    needs a type ascription `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰 : SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ ⥤ Over X)`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED, axiom-clean. `CechComplex` (which is *defined* via `CechNerve`)
  is now also axiom-clean.
- **Definition moved:** `CechNerve` had to be relocated from its original top-of-file
  position (line 83) to just before `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`, because it now depends
  on `coverCechNerve` / `pushPullFunctor` defined later. A forward-pointer comment was
  left at the old location. Blueprint `\lean{}` pin name unchanged.

## Out of scope (left as documented sorry, per objective)
- `CechAcyclic.affine` (now line ~764, P3) — needs from-scratch localisation/contracting-
  homotopy infra; objective said do NOT touch.
- `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` (now line ~801, P5, **protected**) — blocked on the
  two spectral sequences; objective said do NOT touch. Signature untouched.

## Needs blueprint entry
The following NEW declarations have no blueprint block yet (reviewer/planner: add
`\label` + `\lean` + `\uses` so the 1-to-1 Lean↔blueprint correspondence holds). The
chapter already describes the *concepts* in §"three-part construction" (paragraph 2),
so these are concrete realisations of `G` and the backbone-in-`Over X`:

- `AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullFunctor` — the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`.
  Uses: `pushPullObj` (`def:push_pull_obj`), `pushPullMap` (`def:push_pull_map`),
  `pushPullMap_id` (`lem:push_pull_id`), `pushPullMap_comp` (`lem:push_pull_comp`).
- `AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerveOver` — backbone lifted to `SimplicialObject (Over X)`.
  Uses: `coverCechNerve` (`def:cover_cech_nerve`), `CategoryTheory.Over.lift`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerveOverAug` — its augmented form, point = terminal
  `Over.mk (𝟙 X)`. Uses: `coverCechNerveOver`, `CategoryTheory.Over.mkIdTerminal`,
  `SimplicialObject.augment`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechNerveCosimplicial` — the underlying (non-augmented) cosimplicial
  `O_X`-module `coverCechNerveOver.rightOp ⋙ pushPullFunctor F` (documents the
  degree-wise content of `CechNerve`). Uses: `coverCechNerveOver`, `pushPullFunctor`.

Note: `def:cech_nerve` (`CechNerve`) already carries `\uses{def:cover_cech_nerve,
lem:push_pull_id, lem:push_pull_comp}`; the realised dependency now routes through
`coverCechNerveOverAug` + `pushPullFunctor`, so the `\uses` could be refined to point at
the new helpers once they are blueprinted.

## Marker readiness (for sync_leanok / review — I did NOT edit the blueprint)
- `lem:push_pull_comp` (`pushPullMap_comp`): proof block now closes → eligible for `\leanok`.
- `def:cech_nerve` (`CechNerve`): now a complete construction (no `sorry`) → eligible for
  `\leanok` on the statement (already present) and now genuinely backed.
